linewise_data.present_levels_of_performance: Based on assessments, Ethan demonstrates strong visual-motor skills and can accurately copy geometric shapes and letters. He also has excellent auditory memory, being very good at remembering things he hears and enjoying drawing and building with blocks. Academically, his reading skills are developing; he can decode single-syllable words and recognizes high-frequency words, but his fluency is currently below grade-level expectations and he sometimes struggles with comprehending longer, more complex texts, often reading them very slowly and not fully grasping the meaning. In mathematics, he demonstrates a good understanding of number recognition and counting up to 100 and can perform basic addition and subtraction within 20. However, word problems and understanding concepts of measurement are areas where he requires more targeted instruction, as he gets a bit lost when there are too many words involved, and telling time or measuring things isn't his strongest suit yet. Functionally, Ethan is generally a cooperative student. He interacts well with his peers during structured activities and can follow multi-step directions when they are presented clearly and concisely. He is independent in his self-care routines, such as dressing and hygiene; he's generally a happy child and gets along well with others and doesn't typically need much help with personal care. An area for focus is his ability to manage his emotions during transitions and when faced with challenging academic tasks; he has instances of frustration or withdrawal when he encounters difficulties, becoming quite upset if he's struggling with something, and sometimes he just wants to give up or walk away.
